step_by_step_thinking:
Answer:

The lateral pterygoid muscle plays a key role in elevating the mandible during opening and contributes to the posterior displacement of the condyle. This movement limits distolingual extension of the mandibular impression because it resists excessive anterior-posterior or lingual displacement, particularly when the jaw is in centric occlusion.
raw_response:
Answer:

The lateral pterygoid muscle plays a key role in elevating the mandible during opening and contributes to the posterior displacement of the condyle. This movement limits distolingual extension of the mandibular impression because it resists excessive anterior-posterior or lingual displacement, particularly when the jaw is in centric occlusion.  

Answer: C: Lateral pterygoid muscle
